seo.title: Angular implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping Angular projects safely.

content.intro: A technical verification list for deploying enterprise-grade Angular applications, focusing on modern standalone architecture, performance budgets, and RxJS memory safety.

items.title: Enable Production Build Flags
items.description: Verify that 'optimization', 'aot', and 'buildOptimizer' are set to true in the production configuration within angular.json.

items.title: Configure Bundle Size Budgets
items.description: Define 'budgets' in angular.json for the 'initial' bundle type with a maximum warning of 500kb and error at 1mb to prevent bundle bloat.

items.title: Enforce Lazy Loading for Feature Routes
items.description: Audit the AppRoutingModule to ensure all top-level feature routes use 'loadComponent' or 'loadChildren' instead of direct imports.

items.title: Implement NgOptimizedImage
items.description: Replace all standard tags with the 'NgOptimizedImage' directive and provide 'priority' attributes for LCP elements.

items.title: Tree-shake Third-party Libraries
items.description: Run 'source-map-explorer' on the production build to identify and remove unused modules from heavy libraries like Lodash or Moment.js.

items.title: Migrate to Standalone Components
items.description: Convert all components to 'standalone: true' and eliminate 'NgModule' declarations to reduce architectural complexity and improve tree-shaking.

items.title: Standardize Dependency Injection
items.description: Use the 'inject()' function in components and services instead of constructor injection for better type inference and cleaner inheritance.

items.title: Utilize Angular Signals for Local State
items.description: Replace local 'BehaviorSubject' or 'OnPush' manual triggers with Angular Signals to optimize the change detection cycle and reduce overhead.

items.title: Global Service Provisioning
items.description: Verify that all singleton services use 'providedIn: root' to ensure they are tree-shakable and correctly scoped for the entire application.

items.title: Strict Type Checking
items.description: Set 'strict': true in tsconfig.json to enforce null checks and strict property initialization across the codebase.

items.title: Prevent Observable Memory Leaks
items.description: Ensure every manual '.subscribe()' call in components is paired with 'takeUntilDestroyed' or an explicit 'unsubscribe' in ngOnDestroy.

items.title: Prefer Async Pipe over Manual Subscriptions
items.description: Audit templates to use the '| async' pipe for data rendering, delegating subscription management to the framework.

items.title: Implement SwitchMap for Race Conditions
items.description: Verify that search and filter inputs use 'switchMap' rather than 'mergeMap' to cancel obsolete HTTP requests during rapid user input.

items.title: Define Strong API Interfaces
items.description: Replace all instances of 'any' in HttpClient calls with specific TypeScript interfaces representing the backend data contracts.

items.title: Centralized Error Handling
items.description: Implement a global 'HttpInterceptor' to catch 401, 403, and 500 status codes and route them to a consistent error handling service.

items.title: Sanitize Dynamic HTML
items.description: Verify that any content passed to [innerHTML] is processed through Angular's 'DomSanitizer' to prevent XSS attacks.

items.title: Configure XSRF Protection
items.description: Configure 'HttpClientXsrfModule' with 'cookieName' and 'headerName' matching the backend's CSRF implementation.

items.title: Secure Environment Variables
items.description: Check that environment.prod.ts does not contain hardcoded secrets; use build-time replacement for sensitive API keys.

items.title: Route Guard Implementation
items.description: Apply 'CanActivate' guards to all protected routes to ensure client-side authorization is enforced before component initialization.

items.title: Enable Content Security Policy
items.description: Add a CSP meta tag or header that restricts script execution to trusted domains and prevents inline script execution.

items.title: Unit Test Business Logic
items.description: Achieve 80% coverage on services containing complex transformation logic using Jest or Karma.

items.title: Critical Path E2E Coverage
items.description: Implement Cypress or Playwright tests for the 'Happy Path' of the primary user workflows (e.g., Login, Checkout, Data Submission).

items.title: Mock External Dependencies
items.description: Ensure all component tests use 'TestBed' to provide mock implementations of services to isolate the unit under test.

items.title: Automated Linting Gate
items.description: Configure 'ng lint' as a required check in the CI/CD pipeline to prevent code style regressions.

items.title: Accessibility (A11y) Audit
items.description: Run 'axe-core' or the Angular CDK A11y module to verify that all interactive elements have correct ARIA labels and keyboard focus states.

items.title: Stream AI Responses
items.description: Utilize RxJS 'fromFetch' or 'EventSource' to handle streaming responses from LLM endpoints to provide real-time UI updates.

items.title: AI Loading State UX
items.description: Implement specific 'Signal'-based loading indicators for AI inference tasks to manage user expectations during high-latency operations.

items.title: Error Boundaries for AI Services
items.description: Wrap AI-driven components in error boundaries to ensure that a failure in the LLM service does not crash the entire application shell.

items.title: Result Caching Strategy
items.description: Implement a caching layer (e.g., in NgRx or a Service) for common AI queries to minimize token costs and improve response times.

items.title: UI Distinction for AI Content
items.description: Apply a specific CSS class or icon to any data generated by AI to comply with transparency requirements for enterprise users.
